items.headline: Major mixed-use development proposed: 67 homes and retail near Speedy's Tacos
items.summary: Sunnyvale Planning Commission is reviewing a 4.22-acre project that would add 55 townhomes and 12 apartments along with 18,535 square feet of retail space, including a new two-story building. The development also requires subdividing the land into 17 residential lots and would maintain the existing Speedy's Tacos location.

items.headline: City Council updates business license tax rates
items.summary: Campbell revised its business license tax rates through a municipal code amendment. The annual rate adjustment affects local businesses operating in the city.

items.headline: Council addresses affordable housing restrictions if subsidies expire
items.summary: The City Council discussed how affordability restrictions on affordable housing would be maintained if project-based subsidy contracts expire or properties face foreclosure. This policy decision affects long-term protections for subsidized affordable units in San José.

items.headline: FIFA World Cup 2026: Santa Clara approves giant stadium signs
items.summary: The city authorized temporary supergraphic signs on building surfaces near Levi's Stadium during FIFA World Cup 2026 events. This marks a notable visual change to the stadium area during the international tournament.

items.headline: Milpitas approves FY 2026-27 development fees and user charges
items.summary: City Council approved updated development impact fees and user fees for the upcoming fiscal year. This affects costs for new construction projects and city services, potentially impacting housing development timelines and affordability in Milpitas.

items.headline: Santa Clara County homelessness plan presented to Milpitas council
items.summary: The County of Santa Clara presented its Community Plan to End Homelessness to the City Council. This regional initiative may influence Milpitas' local homeless services strategy and resource allocation.

items.headline: Comm Hill Phase 3.1: Five new homes approved on Altino Boulevard
items.summary: San José approved five single-family homes in the Comm Hill development's third phase, ranging from $487k to $573k, all issued on the same day. This represents continued residential infill in an established neighborhood development.

items.headline: City releases guide to building permits, timelines, and coordination
items.summary: San José released a comprehensive guide titled "From Ideation to Occupancy" explaining the building permitting process, expected timelines, and how different city departments coordinate. This resource could help residents and developers understand what to expect when planning construction projects in the city.
